## Notes

### Source-grounded notes
- INTRODUCTION TO AUTONOMOUS MOBILE ROBOTS Course Code: BMA 213 Credits: 3 Contact Hours: L-2 T-0 P-2 Semesters: 4 Course Category: DEC Introduction: Introduction to Autonomous Mobile Robot provides a complete and concise 
- To familiarize the students with mobile robots, basic methods for achieving mobility and autonomy.
- Prerequisite: Basic Mechanical Engineering and Engineering Mechanics Course Outcomes: After successful completion of the course, the student will be able to: CO1: Understand the difference between mobile robots and manip
- CO2: Analyze the kinematic equations for various wheel drives and degree of maneuverability of various drives and kinematic control concepts.
- CO3: Evaluate the dynamic of various mobile robots CO4: Create the model and discuss the challenges involved in sensory perception for mobile robots.

_LLM generation is currently unavailable, so these notes use cleaned retrieved context only._
